etymology.description: Phonetic: Ńá» + Semantic: ščó (arrow) = combines phonetic element with semantic indicator
etymology.interpretation: The character ščú(yăÉ) acts as a final particle in classical Chinese, indicating a change or completed action. The combination of components uses the phonetic Ńá» and semantic ščó (shăÉ, 'arrow') to suggest completedness, akin to reaching a target.
